steps.title: Find the original center
steps.content:
A circle in the form $(x-h)^2+(y-k)^2=r^2$ has center $(h,k)$. For
$$(x+5a)^2+(y-34a)^2=25a^2$$
the center is $(-5a,34a)$.

steps.title: Shift the center right
steps.content:
Shifting $10a$ units to the right adds $10a$ to the $x$-coordinate.
$$-5a+10a=5a$$
The new center is $(5a,34a)$.

steps.title: Write the new equation
steps.content: Keep the same radius and use the new center $(5a,34a)$. $$(x-5a)^2+(y-34a)^2=25a^2$$ The answer is D.
